h1: CertainTeed Landmark vs GAF Timberline HDZ: Side-by-Side Comparison (2026)
lead_summary: CertainTeed Landmark is the heavier of the two and carries the longest algae warranty in its base spec. GAF Timberline HDZ is lighter, easier to install with the wide StrikeZone nailing area, and offers WindProven unlimited wind coverage with a full GAF system. On material cost, Landmark runs about $3 to $5 more per square; on installed cost, that often grows to $100 to $200 per square depending on contractor labor.
choose_a: You want the heaviest architectural shingle in its class for that thicker, more substantial look on the roof.
You prefer transparent, well-documented warranty terms (StreakFighter and the standard 10-year defect coverage).
Your contractor is a CertainTeed SELECT ShingleMaster for the extended workmanship coverage.
You want the widest color variety in a refined two-palette system (Heritage colors and Designer collections).
You buy through ABC Supply or other CertainTeed-loyal distributors.
choose_b: You want WindProven unlimited wind coverage when installed as a full GAF system (no maximum wind speed cap).
You want StainGuard Plus, with copper-infused granules and a 25-year algae warranty (longer than Landmark's StreakFighter baseline).
Your contractor is GAF Master Elite for the Golden Pledge lifetime workmanship warranty.
You want the StrikeZone nailing area for faster, more forgiving installs.
Home Depot is closer than a CertainTeed distributor and you are sourcing materials locally.

cost_delta.notes: Material cost is within $3 to $5 per square. Installed delta widens when CertainTeed's heavier weight increases labor time and required underlayment specs. On a 30-square home, expect the installed difference to be $100 to $200 per square ($3,000 to $6,000 total) in markets where CertainTeed labor runs a premium.

faq_items.q: Which is more durable, CertainTeed Landmark or GAF Timberline HDZ?
faq_items.a: Landmark is heavier (230 lbs/sq vs 240 lbs/sq for HDZ) but HDZ's LayerLock laminate is the more aggressive bonding mechanism. In practice, both routinely last 25 to 35 years when installed correctly with proper ventilation.

Both have lifetime-limited material warranties. HDZ's algae coverage is significantly longer (25 years vs 10 years for Landmark's base StreakFighter). For workmanship, both rely on the contractor's certification level (CertainTeed SELECT ShingleMaster vs GAF Master Elite).

faq_items.q: Which is better for Florida wind?
faq_items.a: GAF Timberline HDZ. The WindProven Limited Wind Warranty has no maximum wind speed cap when installed with four required GAF accessories. Florida installers in hurricane zones tend to slightly prefer HDZ for this reason.

faq_items.q: Is one heavier than the other?
faq_items.a: HDZ at 240 lbs/sq is slightly heavier than Landmark at 230 lbs/sq. The difference is hard to see by eye but does affect installation labor and underlayment spec on lower-pitched roofs.

GAF Timberline HDZ has 33 colors across HD and Bold Definition collections versus Landmark's 24. CertainTeed organizes its colors into a tighter two-palette system (Heritage and Designer) which is easier to navigate but offers fewer choices.

faq_items.q: How does the install difficulty compare?
faq_items.a: HDZ is generally faster to install thanks to the wide StrikeZone nailing area. Crews report ~30 percent faster installs on HDZ. Landmark's heavier construction takes slightly longer to set.
